Chemistry: moles

One mole contains 6.022 x 10^23 particles, known as Avogadro's constant.
The number of moles can be calculated using n = m / M, where m is mass in grams
and M is molar mass in grams per mole. Water has a molar mass of 18 g/mol, so
18 g of water is 1 mol and contains 6.022 x 10^23 water molecules.
